description: Vietnamese tones exhibit allophonic variation based on the voicing of the initial consonant. In the Northern dialect, tones starting with voiceless consonants (p, t, k, m, n, ŋ) are pronounced with a different pitch contour than those starting with voiced consonants (b, d, g, v, z, j), creating a register split that is part of the phonological system.
sources: Thompson, L. C. (1987). _A Vietnamese Grammar_. University of Washington Press.
Huffman, F. E. (1978). _Vietnamese Tone: A New Analysis_. Mouton de Gruyter.
Pham, T. (2003). _Vietnamese tone and register_. In _Studies in Southeast Asian Linguistics_ (pp. 1-24).
